questions.question: Explain the client-server model of resource sharing with a diagram.
keyPoints: Client requests services from server
Server provides resources like files, databases, applications
Server manages resources centrally while clients consume them
Communication via network protocols like TCP/IP

questions.question: List and explain three advantages of resource sharing in computer networks.
keyPoints: Cost reduction through shared hardware/software
Improved resource utilization
Easier centralized management and backup
Enhanced collaboration between users
Simplified maintenance and updates

questions.question: Differentiate between file sharing and database sharing in networks.
keyPoints: File sharing: sharing entire files through protocols like SMB/NFS
Database sharing: sharing structured data with concurrent access management
Database sharing requires DBMS with transaction support
File sharing may lead to inconsistencies while database sharing ensures data integrity

questions.question: What is load balancing? Explain its importance in resource sharing.
keyPoints: Load balancing distributes workload across multiple servers
Prevents single server from being overwhelmed
Improves reliability through redundancy
Enhances response time and user experience
Examples: Round-robin, least-connections algorithms

questions.question: Explain the Peer-to-Peer (P2P) model of resource sharing with its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: P2P is decentralized - each node acts as both client and server
No dedicated server required - reduces cost
Direct resource sharing between peers
Advantages: fault tolerance, scalability, cost-effective
Disadvantages: security challenges, inconsistent performance, management complexity

questions.question: What is network transparency? How does it benefit users in a distributed system?
keyPoints: Network transparency hides network complexity from users
Users access remote resources without knowing their location
Appears as if resources are local
Simplifies user interaction and application development
Types: access, location, migration, replication transparency

questions.question: Explain three types of resources that can be shared in computer networks with examples.
keyPoints: Hardware resources: printers, storage devices, scanners, CPUs
Software resources: applications, databases, operating systems
Data resources: files, documents, multimedia content
Network resources: bandwidth, internet connection

questions.question: Describe the architecture of client-server resource sharing. Discuss its advantages, disadvantages, and suitable applications.
keyPoints: Architecture: Clients request, Server provides centralized resources
Communication via network protocols
Advantages: Centralized security, easier backup, resource control
Disadvantages: Server bottleneck, single point of failure, scalability limits
Applications: Web hosting, database systems, email services, enterprise applications
Scalability through load balancing and clustering

questions.question: Explain the concept of distributed computing as a resource sharing paradigm. Discuss Grid computing and Cloud computing in this context.
keyPoints: Distributed computing shares computation across networked computers
Grid computing: pool of distributed resources for large-scale computing
Cloud computing: on-demand access to shared computing resources
Cloud models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Virtualization enables resource elasticity
Benefits: scalability, cost-efficiency, flexibility
Examples: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud

questions.question: Compare and contrast Peer-to-Peer and Client-Server models of resource sharing. Include architecture, security, scalability, and use cases.
keyPoints: Architecture: P2P decentralized, Client-Server centralized
P2P: each node is client and server; Client-Server: dedicated servers
Security: Client-Server more secure, P2P challenging
Scalability: P2P scales better, Client-Server limited by server capacity
Reliability: P2P has no single point of failure
Use cases P2P: file sharing (BitTorrent), blockchain
Use cases Client-Server: web apps, databases, enterprise systems

questions.question: Discuss network storage solutions for resource sharing. Explain NAS and SAN with their differences and applications.
keyPoints: Network storage provides centralized data storage
NAS: Network Attached Storage - file-level access over TCP/IP
SAN: Storage Area Network - block-level access, high performance
NAS: simple, cost-effective, file sharing
SAN: complex, expensive, high-speed for databases
Applications: backup, virtualization, enterprise storage
Both enable data sharing across network
